prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `bbb1b8b6`.
codex_response:
Implemented `bbb1b8b6` as a new ARC2 task package. The actual rule is stricter than the summary hints suggested: the output is the left `4x4` stencil unchanged unless the right-hand nonzero mask exactly equals the left-hand zero mask; only then are those holes filled with the right color.

I treated the hint as only partially usable. `arc2_opus46_summary.json` had the right decomposition, but both summaries missed the exact-mask-equality gate, so I documented the correction in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_bbb1b8b6/notes.md). No new DSL primitive was needed.

Official verification passed: `9/9`. Generated verification passed: `1000/1000`. I also built the artifacts and visually checked the preview sheets against the originals.

The generator stays close to the task family by keeping the fixed `4x9` layout, using curated `4x4` binary stencil motifs with symmetry variation, weighting fill colors toward the official palette (`2/3/6/7`), and mixing exact-complement cases with structured near-miss distractor masks.
